tasks.prompt: I want to start a YouTube channel about cooking but my first videos aren't getting views. Help me build a content strategy.

checks.desc: Names a specific viewer and a recurring promise, not 'everyone'
Defines a differentiated angle, not just a topic
Hook lands in the first 1-3 seconds with a pattern interrupt
Maps to content pillars and a repeatable series/format
Judges success on retention, not just likes/views
Hook is an honest promise, no clickbait or engagement-bait

tasks.golden: First, before any more filming, get the positioning right: name one specific viewer (say a busy parent who wants 15-minute weeknight dinners) and the recurring promise every video keeps, instead of cooking for everyone. Then find a differentiated angle, something the incumbents in the niche can't or won't say. For each video, the hook has to land in the first 1-3 seconds with a pattern interrupt in the first frame and an open loop; write 5-10 hook variants and pick the strongest, cutting any 'hey guys welcome back' throat-clearing. Build 3-5 content pillars tied to the promise and a repeatable series viewers come back for. Judge success on retention metrics that matter, like intro hold, watch-through, and saves and shares, not just likes and views. Keep every title and thumbnail an honest promise the video pays off, because audience trust is the asset that compounds.
tasks.decoy: Just post a lot and the algorithm will sort it out. Cook whatever you feel like for everyone, copy whatever is trending, and to get views you should use clickbait titles that overpromise even if the video doesn't deliver. Only the view count matters, so don't overthink hooks or who it's for.
